metadata.name: EveryStamp
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A Python interface for retrieving FITS or image cutouts from a variety of astronomical surveys.

modules.name: everystamp.plotters
documentation: Sub-module for plotting FITS images.

functions.name: everystamp.plotters.find_rms
documentation:
from Cyril Tasse/kMS

```
param image_data

image data array

return

rms (noise measure)
```

classes.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicFITSPlot

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicFITSPlot.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a basic plotting object for 2D FITS files.

Args:
    fitsname : str
        Name of the FITS file that will be plotted.

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicFITSPlot.plot2D
documentation:
Save a 2D plot of the loaded FITS file.

Args:
    plot_colourbar : bool
        Add a colour bar to the plot.
    contour_image:
        Add contours based on this image.
    contour_levels:
        Number of contour levels to draw if an integer or contour levels if a list. Defaults to 5.

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicFITSPlot.plot_noaxes
documentation: Save a plot of the FITS image without any axes.

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicFITSPlot.savedata
documentation:
Save data of a BasicPlot object to a FITS file with the same WCS information.

Any tonemapping applied to the original data will be carried over to the FITS file. Physical units will thus be lost.
Creates a basic plot of a FITS file.

classes.name: everystamp.plotters.SRTPlot

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.SRTPlot.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a basic plotting object for 2D FITS files.

Args:
    fitsname : str
        Name of the FITS file that will be plotted.

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.SRTPlot.plot2D
documentation:
Save an SRTPLOT style plot of the loaded FITS file.

Args:
    lines : int
        Number of lines to plot.
    offset : float
        Offset between each line in data units.
Create a line profile plot similar in style to the SRTPLOT task used by Hogbom 1974.

classes.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicImagePlot

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicImagePlot.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a basic plotting object for 2D FITS files.

Args:
    fitsname : str
        Name of the FITS file that will be plotted.

methods.name: everystamp.plotters.BasicImagePlot.plot2D
documentation: Save a plot of the FITS image without any axes.
Creates a basic plot of a FITS file.

modules.name: everystamp.tonemapping
documentation: Sub-module for tone mapping images.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.make_nonnegative
documentation:
Makes an array non-negative by adding the minimum entry to all entries.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Array to make non-negative.

Returns:
    data_scaled : numpy.ndarray
        Non-negative version of data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.normalise
documentation:
Normalise an array of values according to y = (x - vmin / (vmax - vmin).

Args:
    x : numpy.ndarray
        Array of values to normalise.
    vmin : float
        Lower bound for normalisation.
    vmax : float
        Upper bound for normalisation.
    clip : bool
        Clip values to the range [0:1]?

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.gamma
documentation:
Apply a gamma correction to the image, raising it to the power 1/gamma.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Data which to gamma correct.

Returns:
    gamma : float
        The gamma correction factor.

modules.name: everystamp.cutters
documentation: Sub-module for trimming FITS images.

functions.name: everystamp.cutters.make_cutout_2D
documentation:
---
param image: image name
param outfile: output fits file
param pos: central position
param size: image size
---

# Make 2D cutout with astropy

functions.name: everystamp.cutters.make_cutout_region
documentation:
---
param image: image name
param outfile: output fits file
param region: region file
---

# Make 2D cutout with pyregion

functions.name: everystamp.downloaders.flatten
documentation:
Flatten a nested list, as per the example on https://stackoverflow.com/a/40857703.

Args:
    xs : list
        Nested list to flatten.

Returns:
    x : list
        Flattened list.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.FileDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.FileDownloader.get_url_filename
documentation:
Discover file name from HTTP URL, If none is discovered derive name from http redirect HTTP content header Location
\:param url: Url link to file to download
\:type url: str
\:return: Base filename
\:rtype: str

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.FileDownloader.download_file
documentation:
Stream downloads files via HTTP
\:param url: Url link to file to download
\:type url: str
\:param filename: filename overrides filename defined in Url param
\:type filename: str
\:param target_dir: target destination directory to download file to
\:type target_dir: str
\:return: Absolute path to target destination where file has been downloaded to
\:rtype: str

From https://medium.com/better-programming/python-progress-bars-with-tqdm-by-example-ce98dbbc9697

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.LoTSSDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.LoTSSDownloader.format_url
documentation:
Args:
    ra: right ascension in HH:MM:SS format.
    dec: declination in HH:MM:SS format.
    size: size of the cutout area in degrees.

Returns:
    url: the formatted URL.
Downloader sub-class for the LOFAR Two-metre Sky Survey.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.LegacyDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.LegacyDownloader.format_url
documentation: Returns a properly formatted URL that can be used to obtain a cutout from Legacy.
Downloader sub-class for the DESI Legacy Imaging Surveys.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.PanSTARRSDownloader
documentation: Downloader sub-class for the VLASS survey.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.get_tiles
documentation:
Read tiles from tile catalog. If file missing, try wget https://archive-new.nrao.edu/vlass/VLASS_dyn_summary.php

Parameters
----------
summary_file : str
    Location where the VLASS summary file is located.

Returns
-------
tab : astropy Table
    Table containing tile information.

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.search_tiles
documentation:
Search the tile catalog for tiles containing the input coordinate

Parameters
----------
tiles : astropy Table
    Tile catalogue as obtained from get_tiles()
c : SkyCoord
    Location to check for coverage in one of the observed tiles.

Returns
-------
tile name : str
    Best matching tile.
observing epoch : str
    Epoch the best matching tile was observed in.
observing date : str
    Date the best matching tile was observed at.

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.get_subtiles
documentation:
For a given tile name, get the subtile filenames in the VLASS directory

Parse those filenames and return a list of subtile RA and Dec.
RA and Dec returned as a SkyCoord object

Parameters
----------
tilename : str
    Name of the tile to extract a subtile from.
epoch : str
    The epoch the tile was observed in.
consider_QA_rejected : bool
    Also consider tiles that did not pass the quality assurance checks.

Returns
-------
fname : str
    Name of the subtile.
c : astropy SkyCoord
    Coordinate of the subtile.

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.get_cutout
documentation:
Get a smaller cutout from the subtile.

Parameters
----------
imname : str
    Name of the image to make a cutout from.
c : astropy SkyCoord
    Coordinate around which to make a cutout.
crop_scale : int
    Size of the cutout in pixels.

Returns
-------
output_fits : str
    Name of the output FITS file.

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.search_vlass
documentation:
Searches the VLASS catalog for a source

Parameters
----------
c : astropy SkyCoord
    Coordinate to search for in tiles.
crop : bool
    Make a cropped cutout of the area of interest.
crop_scale : int
    Crop the cutout to this amount of pixels centred around c.
consider_QA_rejected : bool
    Also consider tiles that failed the Quality Assurance checks.
ddir : str
    Location to download the cutout to.

Returns
-------
imname : str
    Name of the output image.

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VLASSDownloader.download
documentation:
Download a cutout from the VLASS survey.

Parameters
----------
ra : float
    Right ascension of the coordinate of interest.
dec : float
    Declination of the coordinate of interest.
size : float
    Size of the area of interest in degrees.
ms : str
    Path to a Measurement Set to take coordinates from instead of using ra and dec.
crop : bool
    Crop the image to the area of interest.
consider_QA_rejected : bool
    Also consider tiles that failed the Quality Assurance checks.
ddir : str
    Location to download the cutout to.

Downloader sub-class for the VLASS survey.

Based on the original code by Anna Ho (https://github.com/annayqho/Query_VLASS) and edits by R. Timmerman.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.VODownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.VODownloader.download
documentation:
Download a cutout from the VLASS survey.

Parameters
----------
ra : float
    Right ascension of the coordinate of interest in degrees.
dec : float
    Declination of the coordinate of interest in degrees    .
size : float
    Size of the area of interest in degrees.
ddir : str
    Location to download the cutout to.
Downloader sub-class for surveys offeret through a VO.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.HiPSDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.HiPSDownloader.download
documentation:
Download a cutout from the VLASS survey.

Parameters
----------
ra : float
    Right ascension of the coordinate of interest in degrees.
dec : float
    Declination of the coordinate of interest in degrees    .
size : float
    Size of the area of interest in degrees.
ddir : str
    Location to download the cutout to.
pixsize : float
    Pixel scale of the survey in arcsec. Default is 1.0 arcsec per pixel.
mode : str
    What image format to download. Can be jpg or fits, default is jpg.
Sub-class to download a file from a HiPS image using hips2fits.

classes.name: everystamp.downloaders.SkyViewDownloader

methods.name: everystamp.downloaders.SkyViewDownloader.download
documentation:
Download a cutout from the VLASS survey.

Parameters
----------
ra : float
    Right ascension of the coordinate of interest in degrees.
dec : float
    Declination of the coordinate of interest in degrees    .
size : float
    Size of the area of interest in degrees.
pixsize
    Pixel size to use for the cutout.
ddir : str
    Location to download the cutout to.
Downloader sub-class for surveys offeret through a VO.

modules.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr
documentation: Sub-module containing wrappers to LuminosityHDR's HDR tonemapping programs.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.run_command
documentation:
Run a command through subprocess.

Args:
    cmd : list
        List of arguments building up the command or a string of the full command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr._load_tonemapped_tmpdata
documentation:
Load the tonemapped output of LuminanceHDR ad return it as an array.

Args:
    name : str
        File path to the data to load.
    as_gray : bool
        Treat the image as gray scale or not. Default: True.

Returns:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        NumPy array containing the image.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr._store_tmpfile
documentation:
Store the data to tonemap to a temporary FITS file to pass to LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        NumPy array containing the image.

Returns:
    path : str
        Absolute path to the temporary file.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.ashikhmin
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Ashikmin 2002.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    eq2 : bool
        Default: True.
    simple : bool
        Default: True.
    local_threshold : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.drago
documentation:
Tonemap the image using gradient domain compression as described in Fattal et al. 2002.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    bias : float
        Bias the logarithmic base towards lower or higher values. Default: 0.85.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.duran
documentation:
Tonemap the image using gradient domain compression as described in Durand and Dorsey et al. 2002.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    sigma_spatial : float
        Spatial kernal size. Default: None.
    sigma_range : float
        Range kernel size. Default: None.
    base_contrast : float
        Base contrast. Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.fattal
documentation:
Tonemap the image using gradient domain compression as described in Fattal et al. 2002.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    alpha : float
        Controls which gradient magnitude is preserved when beta is 1. Default is None.
    beta : float
        Controls local detail enhancement.
    colour_saturation : float
        Controls colour saturation.
    noise : float
        Controls the threshold for what is seen as noise, where detail enhancement is reduced.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.ferradans
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the method described in Ferradans et al. 2011.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    rho : float
        Controls overall lightness. Larger values yield a brighter image. Default is -2.
    beta : float
        Controls detail enhancement. Larger values yield more detail enhancement. Default is 5.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.ferwerda
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the method described in Ferwerda et al. 1996.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    multiplier : float
        Default is None.
    luminance_adaptation : float
        Default is None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.kimkautz
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Kim and Kaus 2008.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    c1 : float
        Default is None.
    c2 : float
        Default is None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.lischinski
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the method described in Lischinski et al. 2006.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    alpha : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.mantiuk06
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Mantiuk 2006.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    contrast : float
        Default is None.
    saturation : float
        Default is None.
    detail : float
        Default is None.
    contrast_equalisation : bool
        Default is False.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.mantiuk08
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Mantiuk 2008.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    contrast_enhancement : float
        Default is None.
    colour_saturation : float
        Default is None.
    luminance_level : float
        Used when set_luminance is True. Default is None.
    set_luminance : bool
        Default is False

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.reinhard02
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Mantiuk 2008.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    key : float
        Default: None.
    phi : float
        Default: None.
    use_scales : bool
        Default: True.
    range : float
        Default: None.
    low : float
        Default: None.
    high : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.reinhard05
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Reinhard 2005.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    brightness : float
        Default: None.
    chroma : float
        Default: None.
    lightness : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.pattanaik
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the human vision based method described in Pattanaik et al. 2000 and Pattanaik et al. 2002.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    multiplier : float
        Default: None.
    local_tonemap : bool
        Default: True.
    auto_lum : bool
        Default: True.
    cone_level : float
        Default: None.
    rod_level : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

functions.name: everystamp.tonemapping.lhdr.vanhateren
documentation:
Tonemap the image using the method described in van Hateren 2006.

Parameters set to None will take their default values as set in LuminanceHDR.

Args:
    data : numpy.ndarray
        Input data to tonemap.
    pupil_area : float
        Default: None.

Returns:
    data_tm : numpy.ndarray
        Tonemapped data.

modules.name: everystamp.everystamp
documentation: Python library aiming to provide a wrapper around various astronomical surveys that offer cutouts.
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp.flatten
documentation:
Generator to flatten a list of nested lists.

Args:
    xs : list
        The list to flatten.

Yields:
    x : iterable
        An iterable that will generate the flattened list.
HAS_LHDR = shutil.which('luminance-hdr-cli')

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._add_args_download
documentation:
Add arguments to the argparse instance for downloading cutouts.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._add_args_plot
documentation:
Add arguments to the argparse instance for plotting images.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._add_args_cutout
documentation:
Add arguments to the argparse instance for making cutouts.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._process_args_download
documentation:
Process arguments to the argparse instance for downloading cutouts.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._process_args_plot
documentation:
Process arguments to the argparse instance for plotting images.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp._process_args_cutout
documentation:
Process arguments to the argparse instance for downloading cutouts.

Args:
    parser : ArgumentParser
        ArgumentParser instance to which to add entries.

functions.name: everystamp.everystamp.main
documentation: Main entry point if called as a standalone executable.

variables.name: everystamp.everystamp.HAS_LHDR
documentation: True if not subprocess.getstatusoutput('luminance-hdr-cli') else False
